item.title: Licensing Junior Staff Post - Info
item.content:encoded: The image in this article has been sourced to http://www.wonders-world.com/2011/08/worlds-most-bizzare-bakery-in-thailand.html?m=1 and is not compliant with our license. IT has beenremoved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, this is good body horror with an intriguing "beyond the stars" aspect to it. Late upvote. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anomalous media, a contestant for the most squickiest picture on scp wiki, literal couch potatoes with shades of 1981 and 093.

+1, no other way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new version is definitely an improvement for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure how I feel about this. The description I thought had some weird tonal stuff going on. It just didn't feel like an SCP article somehow. The interview is great, though perhaps because I read it after the transcripts. That the Pastor seems to know what effects his sermons have is the thing I can't really decide if I like, though I do like the religious stuff within the sermons; they paint an intriguing picture. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some changes to the last transcript. Hopefully it's an improvement!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea behind the last transcript - I'm a sucker for that concept, and I liked the first line - but it wasn't done well enough or differently enough to work for me. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with the remark above that the last transcript is a bit trite, and rather a weak way to end what is otherwise a good article. The CoTBG reference (if it is one) also felt a bit gratuitous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first opened the page, I was concerned that the article would be overly reliant on the image. I was pleased to find that this wasn't the case, you did an excellent job constructing this skip, and I believe it could stand on its own even without the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had my face this close to the screen when I clicked on this. Damn, that picture….
Bravo, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the part about Jenny, because it reminds me of some actual televangelists. However, the bit at the end with all the "I have seen these horrible apocalyptic things and will narrate what I saw in a grandiose way" has been done a lot before and feels a bit cliche.

SCP-1981

I've been to the steel mills of Alaska, and the cornfields of Nebraska. I've seen the derelict offices of Google burn with the window boarded up and the squatters inside them. I've seen the houses where they cut up the little babies. From coast to shining coast I have walked empty down drooling path <indecipherable> The decaying flesh of false morality poisoning our children. I have stood atop the mountain of this greedy earth, looking upon our beautiful pious pit, filled to bursting with the vast hands of helplessness. And did you know what I saw? Hell.

This is just one of the ones I remember clearly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh, creepy. Got the real feeling that the Foundationverse has something bigger going on.

Also, 'metal shattered gods' refers to the CotBG, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made some changes to try and address these! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three things:

  • Fusing together of body parts. (fingers, toes, etc.)

I'm not sure, but I think that could be better phrased as "Fusion of body parts(fingers, toes, ect.)."

Later physical alterations often involve the morphing of the body into decidedly non-humanoid forms, often causing the victim to become immobile.

I also feel this could be better phrased, possibly: "Later physical alterations often involve morphing of the body into non-humanoid forms, usually resulting in immobility."

The picture, while creepy, doesn't seem very appropriate for the SCP. Why are the faces hanging on meat hooks?

Otherwise, not bad. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamnit, why must you hit my soft spot for broadcasts?

+1
